items.title: A utm_source / source claim is stored as a CLAIM, never as a classification
items.precondition: Real Express app driven over HTTP via Supertest (real router, real validation middleware, real controller, real own-host filtering). The DB model, AI analysis, recipient routing, and CRM lookup are mocked - the persisted payload is captured and asserted rather than written. No local Postgres exists in this environment (pg_isready and docker are both absent), and the shared dev DB must not take an unmerged migration (AGENTS.md deploy path), so the row write is asserted at the model boundary.
steps: POST /api/websites/form-submission with body utm_source=facebook and an INTERNAL Referer (https://drpavanendo.com/contact).
Capture the object handed to FormSubmissionModel.create.
Assert the label and the provenance columns.
items.expected: 200; the persisted row carries source='facebook' AND source_method='client_label' - the winning claim keeps claim provenance and is not promoted to a classification.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/form-submission-source-route.test.ts A1 passed in the focused 7-test run and the 58-test targeted run.

items.title: A forwarded first-touch referrer is stored as client_referrer (our label, their input)
items.precondition: Same Supertest harness as A1.
steps: POST with body first_touch_referrer=https://www.google.com/ and an INTERNAL Referer.
Capture the FormSubmissionModel.create payload.
items.expected: 200; source='google' AND source_method='client_referrer' - Alloro classified the URL, but the URL arrived from the page, so it is not graded as an observation.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/form-submission-source-route.test.ts A2 passed.
items.notes: This tier is the refinement beyond the review's two-value model: first_touch_referrer arrives in the request BODY, so it is client-supplied input even though the classification is ours.

items.title: A cross-site submit Referer header is stored as header_referrer
items.precondition: Same Supertest harness as A1.
steps: POST with no body source signals and Referer: https://www.bing.com/.
Capture the FormSubmissionModel.create payload.
items.expected: 200; source='bing' AND source_method='header_referrer' - the strongest tier available (browser-set, server-classified), and still not 'verified'.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/form-submission-source-route.test.ts A3 passed.

items.title: THE REVIEW FINDING - an identical label 'google' persists a DIFFERENT source_method per signal
items.precondition: Same Supertest harness as A1. This is the direct behavioral proof of the 5.2 finding: before the fix, a client claim and a server classification were indistinguishable on the row.
steps: POST body source=google with an INTERNAL Referer, then capture the row (the CLAIM path).
Reset the create spy.
POST with no body signals and Referer: https://www.google.com/, then capture the row (the CLASSIFIED path).
Compare source and source_method across the two rows.
items.expected: Both rows carry source='google', but source_method='client_label' vs 'header_referrer' - the two are no longer collapsible, so no report can present the claim as verified attribution.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/form-submission-source-route.test.ts A4 passed. NEGATIVE CONTROL: with the controller's source_method line removed, all 7 items in this file fail with 'AssertionError: expected undefined to be client_label' - the checks discriminate and are not vacuous. The controller was restored byte-identical afterward (diff clean).

items.title: An internal-only journey stores unknown - no source, and no invented provenance
items.precondition: Same Supertest harness as A1. Own hosts resolve to drpavan.sites.getalloro.com and drpavanendo.com.
steps: POST with no body signals and an INTERNAL Referer (https://drpavanendo.com/contact).
Capture the FormSubmissionModel.create payload.
items.expected: 200; source=null AND source_method=null. The invariant (method is null iff source is null) holds - an unknown source never acquires a provenance.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/form-submission-source-route.test.ts A5 passed. The invariant is also property-checked across 8 signal shapes in src/__tests__/sourceAttribution.test.ts ('INVARIANT: method is null if and only if source is null').

items.title: A PII-shaped utm_source reaches neither column
items.precondition: Same Supertest harness as A1.
steps: POST with utm_source=jane.doe:1987-03-04 (a personalized utm carrying a name and DOB).
Capture the FormSubmissionModel.create payload and serialize it.
items.expected: 200; source=null, source_method=null, and the serialized row contains no 'jane.doe' substring - the closed-vocabulary allow-list holds at the HTTP boundary, not only in the unit.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/form-submission-source-route.test.ts A6 passed.
items.notes: Guards the pre-existing PII floor (5.2 / 5.3) against regression from the provenance refactor.

items.title: The row never carries a 'verified' provenance value
items.precondition: Same Supertest harness as A1.
steps: POST with both a utm_source claim and a cross-site Referer.
Assert source_method is one of the three honest tiers and is never 'verified'.
items.expected: source_method is one of client_label, client_referrer, header_referrer; never 'verified'. No submission source is verified attribution - the endpoint is public and every signal is client-influenced (Value #6).
items.evidence: src/__tests__/form-submission-source-route.test.ts A7 passed. Reinforced at the unit layer: isVerifiedAttribution() returns false for every method, and sourceConfidence() returns 'unknown' for any unrecognized stored value rather than upgrading it.

items.title: Migration applies, is reversible, and is idempotent against a real database
items.precondition: NOT RUN - no drivable database in this environment. There is no local Postgres (pg_isready and docker are both absent), and the only reachable DB is the shared dev database, which must not receive an unmerged migration ahead of a dev/dave push (AGENTS.md: 'Migrations run first on dev/dave against the dev database').
items.expected: npm run db:migrate adds source_method VARCHAR(32) NULL to website_builder.form_submissions; db:rollback drops it; re-running up is a no-op via ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS.
items.evidence: Static review only: src/database/migrations/20260716000000_add_source_method_to_form_submissions.ts uses ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS (idempotent) and DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS (reversible), nullable, no default, no index, no backfill - the same pattern as its sibling 20260715000000_add_source_to_form_submissions.ts, which itself mirrors the shipped sender_ip / is_flagged migrations on this table.
items.notes: Deliberately a SEPARATE migration rather than an edit to 20260715000000: that migration may already be recorded in a local or dev knex_migrations table, and knex never re-runs a recorded migration - editing it would mean source_method silently never lands. Production risk: one additive nullable column on one table; no data rewrite, no backfill, no meaningful lock; historical rows stay null, which honestly reads as 'we did not record how'.
items.waiver: WAIVED - un-drivable here, and forcing it would mean migrating the shared dev DB from an unmerged branch, which the deploy path forbids. It executes on the normal path when dev/dave deploys (dev.yml runs npm run db:migrate). Verification owner: the dev deploy - confirm the column exists and that PM2 and /api/health/db stay green.

items.title: An out-of-contract oversized label is DROPPED at the boundary and never reaches the controller
items.precondition: Same Supertest harness as A1. REWRITTEN in Rev 8 for Dave's third-round finding. The prior version of this item asserted warn-only pass-through as CORRECT behavior - it codified the gap rather than closing it. The route now mounts sanitize(attributionInputSchema, ATTRIBUTION_FIELDS) after the warn-only validate(), so the bound is enforced by dropping the field, never by rejecting the request.
steps: POST /api/websites/form-submission with utm_source padded to 200 chars (over the 100-char label cap) and an INTERNAL Referer.
Assert logger.warn recorded the DROP with field names only (never the value).
Capture the FormSubmissionModel.create payload and assert both provenance columns.
items.expected: 200 (the lead is saved); logger.warn is called with fields containing 'utm_source' and the sanitize message, and never logs the value; the persisted row carries source=null, source_method=null, and no trace of the value. The field is deleted from req.body before the controller reads it, so the controller now genuinely can trust it (11.2).
items.evidence: src/__tests__/form-submission-source-route.test.ts A9 passed in the focused 14-test route run, the 99-test targeted run (4 files), and the 544-test full suite (63 files) on Node 22.23.1. NEGATIVE CONTROL: with the sanitize() line unmounted from the route, A9 FAILS (along with A10/A12/A13) - 4 route failures. The route file was restored byte-identical afterward.
items.notes: The honest scope of the Rev 8 fix: the bound now HOLDS, it does not merely log. The closed allow-list remains in place as defense in depth (5.2), but it is no longer the only thing keeping an out-of-contract value out of the row.

items.title: THE REVIEW FINDING - an oversized first_touch_referrer cannot reach persistence
items.precondition: Same Supertest harness as A1. This is the exact case the third-round review named: a 3,000-character Google referrer was rejected by Zod yet still derived {source:'google', method:'client_referrer'}, because warn-only let it reach the controller and hostOf() parses a URL of any length. Verified empirically against the pre-fix code before the fix was written.
steps: POST with first_touch_referrer = 'https://www.google.com/search?q=' + 3000 chars, and an INTERNAL Referer (so no header fallback can mask the result).
Capture the FormSubmissionModel.create payload.
items.expected: 200; source is NOT 'google'; source=null and source_method=null. The over-cap signal is dropped, so the derivation honestly reads unknown instead of laundering an out-of-contract URL into a real channel.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/form-submission-source-route.test.ts A10 passed in the focused 14-test route run, the 99-test targeted run (4 files), and the 544-test full suite (63 files) on Node 22.23.1. NEGATIVE CONTROL: with sanitize() unmounted, A10 fails with 'expected "google" not to be "google"' - reproducing the reviewer's exact finding.
items.notes: This was the one field where the declared bound was neither enforced NOR backstopped: unlike source/utm_source (which the allow-list blocks) the referrer path classifies by hostname, which is unaffected by length. It was the real defect behind the review's finding.

items.title: An oversized source label cannot reach persistence
items.precondition: Same Supertest harness as A1.
steps: POST with source = 'google' padded to 150 chars and an INTERNAL Referer.
Capture the FormSubmissionModel.create payload.
items.expected: 200; source=null, source_method=null, and no trace of the oversized value in the row.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/form-submission-source-route.test.ts A11 passed in the focused 14-test route run, the 99-test targeted run (4 files), and the 544-test full suite (63 files) on Node 22.23.1.
items.notes: HONESTY: this item passes with OR without the sanitize middleware - the closed allow-list already blocked it (normalizeSource truncates to 100 chars, and a 100-char string is never an allow-list key). It is a defense-in-depth lock on the end state, NOT proof of the new guard. A10 and A13 are the discriminating checks.

items.title: THE LEAD IS NEVER LOST - all three attribution fields out of contract, and the submission still persists
items.precondition: Same Supertest harness as A1. This item exists to pin the design decision itself (sanitize, not enforce) as a regression test: if anyone later flips these three fields to hard 400s, this test fails.
steps: POST with source=300 chars, utm_source=300 chars, and a >4000-char first_touch_referrer, plus an INTERNAL Referer.
Assert the HTTP status and that the visitor's actual message is in the persisted row.
items.expected: 200, NOT 400. The persisted row still carries the visitor's contents (Name: 'Sam Rivera'), with source=null and source_method=null. A practice never loses a real patient enquiry because a marketing tool appended a long tracking parameter - the lead is the asset, attribution is a note about the asset.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/form-submission-source-route.test.ts A12 passed in the focused 14-test route run, the 99-test targeted run (4 files), and the 544-test full suite (63 files) on Node 22.23.1. NEGATIVE CONTROL: with sanitize() unmounted, A12 fails (the oversized referrer derives 'google').
items.notes: The answer to the open question Rev 7 raised and the third-round review posed as 'enforce OR sanitize'. Enforce would 400 this request and drop the patient. Sanitize bounds the metadata and keeps the lead, which is why it was chosen.

items.title: Sanitizing holds even when the legacy form schema fails for an unrelated reason
items.precondition: Same Supertest harness as A1. Guards the specific fragility that would have made this fix cosmetic a second time.
steps: POST with hostname padded to 400 chars (over its 255 cap, which busts formSubmissionSchema as a whole) AND a >3000-char first_touch_referrer.
Capture the FormSubmissionModel.create payload.
items.expected: 200; source is NOT 'google'; source=null. Because sanitize() is mounted as its OWN middleware rather than folded into formSubmissionSchema, an unrelated warn-mode parse failure cannot silently switch the guard off.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/form-submission-source-route.test.ts A13 passed in the focused 14-test route run, the 99-test targeted run (4 files), and the 544-test full suite (63 files) on Node 22.23.1. NEGATIVE CONTROL: with sanitize() unmounted, A13 fails - the oversized referrer derives 'google'.
items.notes: Had the three bounds been enforced by folding .catch() into formSubmissionSchema, this request would have gone unsanitized: warn mode only assigns the parsed body back on SUCCESS, so any unrelated field failure would pass the untouched body through. A guard whose operation depends on the rest of the body being clean is a guard that guards nothing - the exact class of error this round was correcting.

items.title: One out-of-contract field does not discard a healthy sibling
items.precondition: Same Supertest harness as A1.
steps: POST with source=300 chars (out of contract) AND utm_source='facebook' (in contract).
Capture the FormSubmissionModel.create payload.
items.expected: 200; source='facebook' and source_method='client_label' - the good claim survives its bad sibling.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/form-submission-source-route.test.ts A14 passed in the focused 14-test route run, the 99-test targeted run (4 files), and the 544-test full suite (63 files) on Node 22.23.1.
items.notes: HONESTY: also passes pre-fix, via the derivation's own fall-through. It locks that sanitize did not REGRESS this - a whole-object .catch() (instead of per-field) would fail here by discarding the good utm_source.

items.title: A non-string attribution value is dropped, not coerced
items.precondition: Same Supertest harness as A1.
steps: POST with utm_source={evil:'object'} and source=12345.
Capture the FormSubmissionModel.create payload.
items.expected: 200; source=null, source_method=null.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/form-submission-source-route.test.ts A15 passed in the focused 14-test route run, the 99-test targeted run (4 files), and the 544-test full suite (63 files) on Node 22.23.1.
items.notes: HONESTY: also passes pre-fix - the controller's own typeof==='string' checks backstop it. Kept as a lock so a future refactor that trusts the boundary (as 11.2 entitles it to) cannot quietly regress it.

items.title: The boundary schema DEFINES source, utm_source, and first_touch_referrer with bounded optional string types (11.2)
items.precondition: Pure schema unit test - formSubmissionSchema.safeParse, no app, no HTTP, no DB. Added in Rev 6. Zod 4.3.6; the asserted issue codes (too_big, invalid_type) were confirmed empirically against the installed zod build, not assumed.
steps: B1: assert each of the three fields raises invalid_type on a non-string, proving it is KNOWN to the schema rather than swallowed by .passthrough().
B2/B3: assert an in-contract payload parses and preserves values, and that all three are optional.
B4/B5: assert a label at exactly 100 chars and a referrer at exactly 2048 chars are in contract, and one char over raises too_big.
B6: assert object/array/number/boolean/null values raise an issue.
B7: assert the issue summary the middleware reads carries field names + codes but never the value.
B8: assert passthrough still tolerates honeypot (_hp/_ts/_jsc) and arbitrary form keys.
items.expected: All 8 checks pass: the three fields are defined and bounded (source/utm_source at SOURCE_LABEL_MAX=100, mirroring MAX_SOURCE_LEN in the capture contract; first_touch_referrer at REFERRER_URL_MAX=2048, the browser URL cap), all remain optional, boundary values are inclusive, and the permissive-first posture for unknown keys is unchanged.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/websiteContact.schemas.test.ts - B1-B8 passed (14 tests in the file incl. the new C-series) in the 544-test full suite (63 files) on Node 22.23.1. NEGATIVE CONTROL (Rev 6, still valid): with the three field definitions stripped, B1/B4/B5/B6/B7 fail; B2/B3/B8 correctly still pass - they are not fix-dependent.
items.notes: B7 guards the same PII floor as A6 at the schema layer: a personalized utm can carry a patient name/DOB, so the zod issue summary must stay value-free before the middleware logs it. The caps are local constants rather than an import from the controller's feature-utils, so validation/ does not depend on a controller (6.2). Rev 8: these definitions now live as single shared constants (sourceLabelField / referrerUrlField) reused by attributionInputSchema, so the contract the warn soak LOGS and the contract sanitize ENFORCES cannot drift apart. formSubmissionSchema still runs warn-only, so B1-B8 describe the contract; C1 proves what holds.

items.title: attributionInputSchema is TOTAL - it can never reject, so sanitizing can never cost a lead
items.precondition: Pure schema unit test (no app, no HTTP, no DB). Added in Rev 8. Zod 4.3.6; .catch(undefined) semantics were confirmed empirically against the installed zod build before the design relied on them.
steps: C1: safeParse a battery of hostile inputs (5000-char source, object utm_source, numeric referrer, null/array/boolean) and assert success===true for every one.
C2: assert an in-contract payload is preserved verbatim.
C3: assert an over-cap value resolves to undefined rather than a truncated string.
C4: assert boundary values exactly AT the caps (100 / 2048) stay in contract.
C5: assert per-field independence - a 400-char source does not discard a valid utm_source.
C6: assert ATTRIBUTION_FIELDS equals the schema's own keys.
items.expected: All 6 pass. The schema never fails (C1), so mounting it via sanitize() cannot reject a submission; out-of-contract values become absent rather than truncated (C3), because a truncated label/URL would be one the visitor never sent; and the key list cannot silently drift from the schema (C6).
items.evidence: src/__tests__/websiteContact.schemas.test.ts C1-C6 - 14 tests passed in the file; part of the 544-test full suite.
items.notes: C1 is the property the whole design rests on: sanitize() can only promise 'bound the field, keep the request' if the schema it parses cannot fail.

items.title: sanitize() middleware - never rejects, drops out-of-contract keys, fails closed on the field
items.precondition: Unit tests against a throwaway one-route Express app (no app.ts dependency), mirroring the existing validate() middleware suite. Added in Rev 8. This middleware is new in this PR.
steps: Assert an in-contract value passes through untouched, and an over-cap value is DROPPED with a 200 (never a rejection).
Assert a wrong-type value is dropped rather than coerced.
Assert only the offending field is dropped - a healthy sibling survives.
Assert UNLISTED keys (honeypot _hp/_ts, arbitrary form fields) are left alone.
Assert the warn log carries the field NAME but never the value (5.3), and that nothing is logged when all fields are in contract.
Assert FAIL-CLOSED: a schema whose safeParse throws causes every listed key to be dropped, while the request is still served 200 and unlisted keys survive.
Assert a request with no parsed body is tolerated.
items.expected: All 9 pass. No status is ever set by the middleware; out-of-contract keys are deleted from req.body so downstream code cannot read them; on an internal error the metadata is lost but the submission is not.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/validate.middleware.test.ts - 'sanitize() - bounded subset, never rejects' block; part of the 99-test targeted run and the 544-test full suite.
items.notes: The fail-closed-on-the-field / fail-open-on-the-request asymmetry is the whole point: on a public lead-capture route, losing a tracking label is acceptable and losing a patient enquiry is not.

items.title: THE REVIEW FINDING (round 4) - a real patient's inquiry survives a hostile attribution value under the enforce flag
items.precondition: Real Express app driven over HTTP via Supertest with VALIDATION_ENFORCE=1 set BEFORE the module graph loads (vi.stubEnv + vi.resetModules + dynamic import of src/app.ts) - `validate` computes its process-wide default mode once at module load, so the flag can only be exercised on a freshly loaded app. Real router, real middleware stack, real controller; the DB model, reCAPTCHA, and the email webhook are mocked, so the persisted payload is captured and asserted rather than written. Added in Rev 9. No local Postgres exists and the shared dev DB must not take an unmerged migration (AGENTS.md), so the row write is asserted at the model boundary.
steps: Set VALIDATION_ENFORCE=1 and load the app fresh.
POST /api/websites/form-submission with a legitimate body (contents Name/Email) and an over-cap utm_source (300 chars), with an INTERNAL Referer.
Capture the object handed to FormSubmissionModel.create.
items.expected: 200; the row carries the visitor's real contents (Name='Sam Rivera', Email='sam@example.com'), and source/source_method are both null - the tracking parameter degraded to honest unknown while the LEAD landed.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/form-submission-enforce-flag.test.ts D1 passed in the 5-test file run, the 104-test targeted run (5 files), and the 549-test full suite (64 files) on Node 22.23.1, exit codes read directly. NEGATIVE CONTROL (mutation-proved, not reasoned): with src/routes/websiteContact.ts reverted to head 482052da (`git checkout HEAD -- src/routes/websiteContact.ts`, removing both `mode: "warn"` pins) the file ran exit 1 with 3 failed | 2 passed. D1 failed with `AssertionError: expected 400 to be 200` - i.e. pre-fix the route answered 400 { code: VALIDATION_ERROR, fields: ["utm_source"], issues: ["too_big"] } and FormSubmissionModel.create was called 0 times. The patient's name and email never reached the database, killed by a tracking parameter they never typed.
items.notes: This is the round-4 finding stated behaviorally. The Rev 8 design (sanitize, not enforce) was correct but incomplete: the three attribution fields were ALSO still declared in formSubmissionSchema, which the route mounted WITHOUT pinning a mode - so it inherited the process-wide VALIDATION_ENFORCE default and became a rejection path the moment that flag was set. Rev 8's Done criterion (b) claimed 'A12 fails if anyone flips these fields to a 400', but A12 runs under the DEFAULT mode only, so it could not see this. Fixed by pinning `mode: "warn"` at the mount.

items.title: All three attribution fields out of contract under the enforce flag - the lead still persists, none reach the row
items.precondition: Real Express app driven over HTTP via Supertest with VALIDATION_ENFORCE=1 set BEFORE the module graph loads (vi.stubEnv + vi.resetModules + dynamic import of src/app.ts) - `validate` computes its process-wide default mode once at module load, so the flag can only be exercised on a freshly loaded app. Real router, real middleware stack, real controller; the DB model, reCAPTCHA, and the email webhook are mocked, so the persisted payload is captured and asserted rather than written. Added in Rev 9. No local Postgres exists and the shared dev DB must not take an unmerged migration (AGENTS.md), so the row write is asserted at the model boundary.
steps: Set VALIDATION_ENFORCE=1 and load the app fresh.
POST with an over-cap source (300 chars), an over-cap utm_source (300 chars), AND a 3,032-char first_touch_referrer, with an INTERNAL Referer.
Capture the FormSubmissionModel.create payload.
items.expected: 200; the row carries the visitor's contents; source and source_method are both null; no fragment of the oversized referrer appears anywhere in the row.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/form-submission-enforce-flag.test.ts D2 passed in the same runs as D1. NEGATIVE CONTROL (mutation-proved, not reasoned): with src/routes/websiteContact.ts reverted to head 482052da (`git checkout HEAD -- src/routes/websiteContact.ts`, removing both `mode: "warn"` pins) the file ran exit 1 with 3 failed | 2 passed. D2 failed with `AssertionError: expected 400 to be 200`.
items.notes: This is Dave's round-3 ask ('add a route regression proving over-limit source, utm_source, and first_touch_referrer cannot reach persistence') re-proved in the world round 3 could not see - with the flag ON. A9/A10/A12 cover the same ground under the default mode and still pass; this is the flag-enabled twin, not a replacement.

items.title: A healthy attribution value still classifies normally under the enforce flag (the pin did not cost the feature)
items.precondition: Real Express app driven over HTTP via Supertest with VALIDATION_ENFORCE=1 set BEFORE the module graph loads (vi.stubEnv + vi.resetModules + dynamic import of src/app.ts) - `validate` computes its process-wide default mode once at module load, so the flag can only be exercised on a freshly loaded app. Real router, real middleware stack, real controller; the DB model, reCAPTCHA, and the email webhook are mocked, so the persisted payload is captured and asserted rather than written. Added in Rev 9. No local Postgres exists and the shared dev DB must not take an unmerged migration (AGENTS.md), so the row write is asserted at the model boundary.
steps: Set VALIDATION_ENFORCE=1 and load the app fresh.
POST with an in-contract utm_source=facebook and an INTERNAL Referer.
Capture the FormSubmissionModel.create payload.
items.expected: 200; source='facebook' AND source_method='client_label' - attribution still works when the value is in contract.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/form-submission-enforce-flag.test.ts D3 passed in the same runs as D1.
items.notes: HONESTY - THIS TEST PASSES WITHOUT THE FIX and is therefore NOT proof of it. It passed in the negative-control run (2 of 5 passed pre-fix; this was one). It cannot fail pre-fix because an in-contract value satisfies formSubmissionSchema, so enforce mode never rejects it. It is a LOCK, not evidence: without it, D1/D2 could be satisfied by a route that ignored attribution entirely. D1, D2, and D4 are the tests that actually fail without the fix.

items.title: /contact - a patient who writes at length is not turned away by the enforce flag (the WIDER instance of the same defect)
items.precondition: Real Express app driven over HTTP via Supertest with VALIDATION_ENFORCE=1 set BEFORE the module graph loads (vi.stubEnv + vi.resetModules + dynamic import of src/app.ts) - `validate` computes its process-wide default mode once at module load, so the flag can only be exercised on a freshly loaded app. Real router, real middleware stack, real controller; the DB model, reCAPTCHA, and the email webhook are mocked, so the persisted payload is captured and asserted rather than written. Added in Rev 9. No local Postgres exists and the shared dev DB must not take an unmerged migration (AGENTS.md), so the row write is asserted at the model boundary. reCAPTCHA verification and the email webhook are mocked for this route.
steps: Set VALIDATION_ENFORCE=1 and load the app fresh.
POST /api/websites/contact with a valid name/phone/email/captchaToken and a message over the schema's 3,000-char cap - a patient describing their dental history in detail.
Assert the response and that the email webhook fired.
items.expected: Not a 400; sendEmailWebhook called exactly once - the practice receives the inquiry.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/form-submission-enforce-flag.test.ts D4 passed in the same runs as D1. NEGATIVE CONTROL (mutation-proved, not reasoned): with src/routes/websiteContact.ts reverted to head 482052da (`git checkout HEAD -- src/routes/websiteContact.ts`, removing both `mode: "warn"` pins) the file ran exit 1 with 3 failed | 2 passed. D4 failed with `AssertionError: expected 400 not to be 400` - pre-fix the route answered 400 { fields: ["message"], issues: ["too_big"] } and the practice never received the inquiry.
items.notes: FOUND BY AUDITING THE FLAG'S BLAST RADIUS, not by the line the review named. /contact is the OTHER public lead-capture route in the same router and had the identical defect: an unpinned `validate` inheriting the process-wide flag. It is arguably worse than the /form-submission case, because websiteContactController imposes NO length cap of its own - so message's 3,000-char schema bound was a NEW rejection that only existed under the flag, and it fires on text the patient actually typed rather than on a tracking parameter. Fixed in the same edit. SCOPE NOTE for the reviewer: this is one line beyond the round-4 sentence. Called out explicitly so it can be rejected if unwanted - it is strictly lead-preserving and changes nothing at today's default (warn).

items.title: The pin is SURGICAL - VALIDATION_ENFORCE still enforces on an unpinned route
items.precondition: Throwaway one-route Express app built against a freshly imported middleware/validate with VALIDATION_ENFORCE=1, mounting validate() UNPINNED exactly as the auth/billing routes do.
steps: Set VALIDATION_ENFORCE=1 and re-import middleware/validate fresh.
Mount validate(z.object({ field: z.string().max(5) })) with no mode option.
POST an over-cap field.
items.expected: 400 with code VALIDATION_ERROR - the flag's default-mode wiring is intact for every route that has not deliberately opted out.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/form-submission-enforce-flag.test.ts D5 passed in the same runs as D1.
items.notes: HONESTY - THIS TEST PASSES WITHOUT THE FIX and is NOT proof of it (it was the other of the 2 that passed in the negative-control run). It tests middleware wiring this revision does not touch. Its job is to block the CHEAP fix: 'make the lead un-rejectable' must not be achieved by neutering VALIDATION_ENFORCE process-wide, because the auth/billing soak still needs to graduate to enforce one day. Kept as a guard against a future regression, not counted as evidence for this one.
